scenario_results.reason: The lane moved a standard commercial signer-and-entitlement request from accepted to completed only after documentary authority, system confirmations, and notice logging aligned.
Missing board-resolution and signer evidence produced a blocked state and prerequisite request rather than speculative fulfillment.
An urgent request to bypass dual control and callback was refused and routed to the proper approver instead of being executed in-lane.
Conflicting CRM, core, document, and communication records triggered rework handling and prevented unsupported closure.
Fraud or AML indicators on a commercial payment-access change stopped advancement and generated an escalation note without investigative overreach.
The lane refused to mark work complete when treasury-platform, approval, and communication evidence were absent.
Commercial-banking terminology such as CIF, secretary certificate, signer matrix, ACH company ID, and Fedwire template was preserved rather than flattened into generic ops language.
Credit-approval and legal-interpretation requests were rejected cleanly and routed to the correct owners.
Missing callback, approval, and product-source context triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Current FinCEN guidance and stricter tenant policy on beneficial-owner handling were recorded as a precedence conflict requiring escalation.
Tenant adaptation preserved separate subsidiary authority and control paths rather than merging distinct commercial-entity workflows.
Requests that became treasury-settlement and loan-servicing work were handed off rather than absorbed by the lane.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and financial-services-and-insurance abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and financial-services-and-insurance contract.
The package stays inside commercial banking operations and does not claim treasury-settlement, fraud-disposition, AML-investigation, legal-interpretation, underwriting, or credit-approval authority.
